input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android 15+ / API 35), edge-to-edge display is enforced, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated. The new approach involves handling keyboard insets properly.

Let me provide a concise answer about the recommended approach for .NET 10 / MAUI.


In .NET 10 with Android 15+, `SoftInput.AdjustResize` is completely ignored due to edge-to-edge rendering enforcement. Instead, I need to use `WindowInsetsCompat` to listen for keyboard (IME) insets and adjust the layout's bottom padding or margin accordingly when the keyboard appears.

For .NET MAUI specifically, there may be built-in handling through new edge-to-edge APIs or keyboard inset behaviors that abstract away the platform-specific details, though the underlying mechanism still relies on the `WindowInsets` approach.

Setting up a platform-specific Android listener using `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` is the most direct way to capture IME insets and adjust padding accordingly. I'm working through the practical implementation for .NET 10 targeting Android 15+, starting with enabling edge-to-edge in the MainActivity...

Still writing the listener implementation...

Actually, I'm reconsidering this approach. In .NET 10 MAUI, the framework might have a more straightforward solution since Android 15 enforces edge-to-edge layouts and deprecates the old `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` behavior. Let me look at what the MAUI team recommends for handling keyboard insets in the newer version.
